Code §§ 5321.08, 5321.17, Chapter 1923","notice_requirements":{"nonpayment_of_rent":"3-day notice to vacate for nonpayment (R.C. 1923.04)","holdover_tenant":"30-day notice to terminate for month-to-month tenancy","lease_violation":"30-day notice for noncompliance (R.C. 5321.11)","drug_criminal_activity":"Immediate termination possible with 30-day notice if tenant allows drug activity (R.C. 5321.17)"},"full_text":"Ohio Rev. Code § 5321.08. Landlord's remedies for tenant's breach. (A) If the tenant fails to comply with the obligations of sections 5321.04 to 5321.05 of the Revised Code or the tenant's obligations under the rental agreement, the landlord may: (1) Recover damages caused by the tenant's breach; (2) Bring an action for rent that is due; (3) Terminate the rental agreement and bring an action for possession under Chapter 1923 of the Revised Code; (4) Take any other action that is appropriate to enforce the landlord's rights.\n(B) If the landlord brings an action for possession under Chapter 1923 and the court finds that the tenant has failed to pay rent when due, the court may issue a writ of restitution placing the landlord in possession of the premises.\nR.C. 1923.04. Notice to vacate. In an action for nonpayment of rent, the landlord shall give the tenant three days' notice to leave the premises. If the premises are used as a dwelling unit by the tenant, the notice shall be served upon the tenant or left at the tenant's usual place of residence at least three days before the hearing."}

{"statutes":"Ohio Rev. Code §§ 5321.06, 5321.16","full_text":"Ohio Rev. Code § 5321.06. Terms and conditions of rental agreement. (A) The landlord and tenant may include in a rental agreement any terms and conditions, including terms and conditions relating to rent and the duration of the agreement, that are not inconsistent with or prohibited by Chapter 5321 or by any other rule of law. (B) Rent increase. If the landlord increases the rent, the tenant may terminate the rental agreement by giving the landlord written notice of termination at least thirty days before the effective date of the increase.\nOhio Rev. Code § 5321.16. Landlord's duties. (A) The landlord shall comply with all applicable provisions of any building, housing, health, and safety codes that materially affect health and safety; (B) make all repairs and do whatever is necessary to put and keep the premises in a fit and habitable condition; (C) keep all common areas of the premises in a safe and sanitary condition; (D) maintain in good and safe working order all electrical, plumbing, sanitary, heating, ventilating, and air conditioning fixtures and appliances; (E) provide and maintain appropriate garbage containers and arrange for garbage removal in the case of four or more units in the building; (F) supply running water and reasonable amounts of hot water at all times and reasonable heat unless the building is not required by law to be equipped for that purpose, or the heat or hot water is generated by an building heating or water-heating system in the premises or by another system under the tenant's exclusive control; (G) not abuse the right of access conferred by section 5321.05 of the Revised Code; (H) give reasonable notice before entry, which shall be presumed to be 24 hours for non-emergency entry; (I) except in the case of a emergency or when the tenant has abandoned or surrendered the premises, may not enter the premises except at a reasonable time. (J) The landlord may not engage in retaliatory conduct under section 5321.02 or 5321.03.\nNote: Ohio eliminated the security deposit interest requirement in 2016 (HB 390). Landlords are no longer required to pay interest on security deposits."}

sources.title: {"statutes":"Ohio Rev. Code §§ 5321.08, 5321.17, Chapter 1923","notice_requirements":{"nonpayment_of_rent":"3-day notice to vacate for nonpayment (R.C. 1923.04)","holdover_tenant":"30-day notice to terminate for month-to-month tenancy","lease_violation":"30-day notice for noncompliance (R.C. 5321.11)","drug_criminal_activity":"Immediate termination possible with 30-day notice if tenant allows drug activity (R.C. 5321.17)"},"full_text":"Ohio Rev. Code § 5321.08. Landlord's remedies for tenant's breach. (A) If the tenant fails to comply with the obligations of sections 5321.04 to 5321.05 of the Revised Code or the tenant's obligations under the rental agreement, the landlord may: (1) Recover damages caused by the tenant's breach; (2) Bring an action for rent that is due; (3) Terminate the rental agreement and bring an action for possession under Chapter 1923 of the Revised Code; (4) Take any other action that is appropriate to enforce the landlord's rights.\n(B) If the landlord brings an action for possession under Chapter 1923 and the court finds that the tenant has failed to pay rent when due, the court may issue a writ of restitution placing the landlord in possession of the premises.\nR.C. 1923.04. Notice to vacate. In an action for nonpayment of rent, the landlord shall give the tenant three days' notice to leave the premises. If the premises are used as a dwelling unit by the tenant, the notice shall be served upon the tenant or left at the tenant's usual place of residence at least three days before the hearing."}

(A) If the tenant fails to comply with the obligations of sections 5321.04 to 5321.05 of the Revised Code or the tenant's obligations under the rental agreement, the landlord may: (1) Recover damages caused by the tenant's breach; (2) Bring an action for rent that is due; (3) Terminate the rental agreement and bring an action for possession under Chapter 1923 of the Revised Code; (4) Take any other action that is appropriate to enforce the landlord's rights.\n(B) If the landlord brings an action for possession under Chapter 1923 and the court finds that the tenant has failed to pay rent when due, the court may issue a writ of restitution placing the landlord in possession of the premises.\nR.C. 1923.04. (A) If the tenant fails to comply with the obligations of sections 5321.04 to 5321.05 of the Revised Code or the tenant's obligations under the rental agreement, the landlord may: (1) Recover damages caused by the tenant's breach; (2) Bring an action for rent that is due; (3) Terminate the rental agreement and bring an action for possession under Chapter 1923 of the Revised Code; (4) Take any other action that is appropriate to enforce the landlord's rights.\n(B) If the landlord brings an action for possession under Chapter 1923 and the court finds that the tenant has failed to pay rent when due, the court may issue a writ of restitution placing the landlord in possession of the premises.\nR.C. 1923.04. Notice to vacate. In an action for nonpayment of rent, the landlord shall give the tenant three days' notice to leave the premises. If the premises are used as a dwelling unit by the tenant, the notice shall be served upon the tenant or left at the tenant's usual place of residence at least three days before the hearing."}

sources.title: {"statutes":"Ohio Rev. Code §§ 5321.04(A)(7)(8), 5321.05","full_text":"Ohio Rev. Code § 5321.05. Tenant's obligations. (A) The tenant shall: ...(7) Not unreasonably refuse to allow the landlord to enter the dwelling unit pursuant to division (B) of this section.\n(B) The landlord may enter the dwelling unit if: (1) The entry is at a reasonable time; (2) The landlord gives the tenant reasonable notice before entering, which shall be presumed to be twenty-four hours; and (3) The entry is for one of the following purposes: (a) To inspect the premises; (b) To make ordinary, necessary, or agreed repairs, decorations, alterations, or improvements; (c) To supply necessary or agreed services; (d) To exhibit the dwelling unit to prospective or actual purchasers, mortgagees, tenants, workmen, or contractors; (e) If the tenant is absent from the premises and the landlord reasonably believes that entry is necessary to protect the premises or property. (C) The tenant shall not abuse the right of access.\nLandlord's remedies for violation: If the tenant violates this section, the landlord may recover actual damages and reasonable attorney fees."}

sources.title: {"statutes":"Ohio Rev. Code §§ 5321.03, 5321.04, 5321.07","full_text":"Ohio Rev. Code § 5321.04. Tenant's obligations. The tenant shall: (1) Comply with all applicable building, housing, health, and safety codes that materially affect health and safety; (2) Keep the premises safe and sanitary; (3) Dispose of garbage properly; (4) Not deliberately or negligently destroy, deface, damage, or remove any part of the premises; (5) Not permit a person to remain in the premises who willfully or carelessly destroys, defaces, or damages the premises; (6) Not engage in or permit drug activity prohibited under Chapter 2925 of the Revised Code.\nOhio Rev. Code § 5321.03. Landlord's obligations. The landlord shall: (1) Comply with all applicable building, housing, health, and safety codes that materially affect health and safety; (2) Make all repairs and do whatever is necessary to put and keep the premises in a fit and habitable condition; (3) Keep all common areas of the premises in a safe and sanitary condition; (4) Maintain in good and safe working order all electrical, plumbing, sanitary, heating, ventilating, and air conditioning fixtures and appliances; (5) Provide garbage containers and arrange for removal (4+ unit buildings); (6) Supply running water and reasonable hot water at all times and reasonable heat unless not required by law; (7) Not abuse access rights; (8) Give reasonable notice before entry (presumed 24 hours); (9) Not engage in retaliatory conduct.\nOhio Rev. Code § 5321.07. Failure of landlord to fulfill obligations. If a landlord fails to fulfill the obligations of § 5321.04, the tenant may: (1) Give written notice to the landlord specifying the failure; (2) After reasonable time (presumed 30 days), deposit rent with the court clerk, apply for an order to compel compliance, or terminate the rental agreement."}

The landlord shall: (1) Comply with all applicable building, housing, health, and safety codes that materially affect health and safety; (2) Make all repairs and do whatever is necessary to put and keep the premises in a fit and habitable condition; (3) Keep all common areas of the premises in a safe and sanitary condition; (4) Maintain in good and safe working order all electrical, plumbing, sanitary, heating, ventilating, and air conditioning fixtures and appliances; (5) Provide garbage containers and arrange for removal (4+ unit buildings); (6) Supply running water and reasonable hot water at all times and reasonable heat unless not required by law; (7) Not abuse access rights; (8) Give reasonable notice before entry (presumed 24 hours); (9) Not engage in retaliatory conduct.\nOhio Rev. Code § 5321.07. Failure of landlord to fulfill obligations. If a landlord fails to fulfill the obligations of § 5321.04, the tenant may: (1) Give written notice to the landlord specifying the failure; (2) After reasonable time (presumed 30 days), deposit rent with the court clerk, apply for an order to compel compliance, or terminate the rental agreement."}
